title: Order service OOM crash during flash sale
root_cause: Memory leak in order-service triggered by sustained traffic >500 rps during flash sale. JVM heap exhausted, causing cascading timeouts to payment-gateway.
resolution_steps: On-call paged at P1 severity via PagerDuty
Identified order-service pods restarting via New Relic container metrics
Confirmed heap exhaustion in JVM metrics dashboard
Scaled order-service from 6 to 12 pods to distribute load
Applied rate limiting at API gateway (300 rps per pod)
Pods stabilized; payment-gateway timeouts cleared within 10 minutes
Post-incident: patched memory leak in cart serialization (PR #3847)

title: Elasticsearch cluster yellow — catalog search degraded
root_cause: Unbalanced shard allocation after a node replacement left 3 shards unassigned. Search latency p99 spiked from 50ms to 1200ms.
resolution_steps: Alert fired on catalog-search latency threshold breach
Checked Elasticsearch cluster health — status yellow, 3 unassigned shards
Ran manual shard reroute to rebalance across available nodes
Monitored recovery — cluster back to green in 45 minutes
Post-incident: added automated shard rebalance check to daily cron

title: Stripe webhook delivery failures causing payment state drift
root_cause: Stripe experienced a partial outage causing webhook delivery delays. Our payment-gateway webhook endpoint hit connection pool exhaustion processing the retry storm when Stripe recovered.
resolution_steps: P1 alert on payment-gateway error rate >5%
Confirmed Stripe status page showing webhook delivery issues
Increased connection pool max from 50 to 150 on payment-gateway
Implemented exponential backoff on webhook processing queue
Ran reconciliation job to sync payment states that drifted during outage
Post-incident: added circuit breaker on webhook ingestion endpoint

title: Membership API cache invalidation causing stale plan data
root_cause: Race condition in cache invalidation during concurrent plan changes. Members upgrading plans saw stale pricing for 2-5 minutes after change.
resolution_steps: Customer support reported pricing inconsistency complaints
Traced to membership-api returning cached plan data after upgrade
Identified race condition: cache TTL reset before DB write committed
Applied hotfix: moved cache invalidation to post-commit hook
Flushed Redis cache for affected member IDs manually
Verified pricing consistency across 50 sample accounts

title: Fulfillment router sending orders to wrong distribution center
root_cause: Stale DC capacity data from warehouse-api (nightly sync failed silently at 3am). Fulfillment router used 18-hour-old capacity numbers, routing 340 orders to the wrong DC.
resolution_steps: Ops team flagged unexpected order volume at NV distribution center
Traced to fulfillment-router capacity cache showing incorrect availability
Found warehouse-api nightly sync had failed with Netsuite timeout
Manually triggered sync, waited for completion (12 minutes)
Re-routed 340 misrouted orders via admin tool
Post-incident: added alerting on nightly sync failure and staleness check

title: User-auth token refresh storm during morning traffic ramp
root_cause: Token refresh race condition under high concurrency. Multiple concurrent requests for the same user triggered parallel token refreshes, overwhelming Redis cluster.
resolution_steps: Redis connection pool alerts fired at 13:00
Identified thundering herd pattern on token refresh endpoint
Applied request deduplication using Redis SETNX as distributed lock
Redis connections normalized within 5 minutes of fix deployment
Post-incident: added token refresh coalescing to auth SDK

title: Notification service SES throttling during promo blast
root_cause: Marketing triggered a 500K member promotional email blast. SES rate limit (200/sec) hit, causing queue backlog. Transactional emails (order confirmations, shipping updates) queued behind promotional batch.
resolution_steps: Alert on notification-service queue depth >10K
Identified SES throttle errors in application logs
Separated promotional and transactional queues (manual config change)
Prioritized transactional queue processing
Promotional emails drained over next 2 hours at throttled rate
Post-incident: implemented priority queue system with dedicated SES pools

title: Inventory API nightly sync timeout
root_cause: Nightly Snowflake sync job processing 2.3M SKU updates timed out at the 90-minute mark. Large batch from new vendor onboarding exceeded normal volume by 3x.
resolution_steps: Alert on inventory sync job duration >90 minutes
Checked Snowflake query history — large batch identified
Increased job timeout to 180 minutes for this run
Job completed successfully at 05:45
Backfilled catalog-search index with updated inventory data
Post-incident: implemented chunked processing for batches >500K SKUs

title: Recommendation engine cold start after deployment
root_cause: Rolling deployment of recommendation-engine caused 45-second cold start per pod while ML model loaded into memory. During rollout, reduced healthy pod count caused latency spikes.
resolution_steps: Latency alert on recommendation-engine p99 >500ms
Confirmed deployment in progress via ArgoCD
Waited for rolling deployment to complete (all pods healthy)
Latency normalized after final pod reached ready state
Post-incident: implemented model pre-loading in readiness probe

title: Fraud detection model serving stale predictions
root_cause: Model retraining pipeline failed silently 3 days prior. Production model was 72 hours stale, causing increased false positives (legitimate orders flagged as fraudulent).
resolution_steps: Customer support escalated high volume of order-blocked complaints
Checked fraud-detection model version — confirmed 72 hours stale
Traced to retraining pipeline failure (S3 data lake partition missing)
Manually triggered retraining with correct partition path
Deployed fresh model, false positive rate returned to baseline within 30 minutes
Post-incident: added model freshness alert (>24h = P2 page)

title: Product ingestion duplicate SKUs from Magento sync
root_cause: Magento catalog sync created 847 duplicate SKUs due to vendor submitting products with inconsistent identifier formatting (hyphens vs underscores in SKU field).
resolution_steps: Alert on product-ingestion error rate >2%
Identified duplicate key violations in ingestion logs
Ran dedup script to merge duplicate SKUs (kept most recent)
Re-indexed catalog-search to remove stale entries
Reconciled inventory counts for affected SKUs
Post-incident: added SKU normalization layer in ingestion pipeline

title: Shipping calculator carrier API timeout cascade
root_cause: FedEx API experiencing degraded response times (>5s vs normal 200ms). Shipping calculator had no circuit breaker, causing thread pool exhaustion as requests queued up.
resolution_steps: Alert on shipping-calculator latency p99 >3s
Confirmed FedEx API degradation via their status page
Enabled fallback to cached shipping rates for FedEx zones
Added circuit breaker with 3s timeout and fallback to UPS/USPS rates
FedEx recovered 45 minutes later; circuit breaker auto-closed
Post-incident: implemented circuit breakers for all carrier API calls

title: Billing service proration calculation error
root_cause: Floating point rounding error in proration calculation for mid-cycle plan changes. Members upgrading from monthly to annual were overcharged by $0.01-$0.03.
resolution_steps: Finance team flagged billing discrepancy in daily reconciliation
Traced to proration logic using float instead of decimal for currency
Patched calculation to use BigDecimal with HALF_UP rounding
Generated refund batch for 1,247 affected members ($28.41 total)
Deployed fix and verified with 100 test proration scenarios

title: Warehouse API Netsuite rate limit during peak sync
root_cause: Netsuite API rate limit (300 req/min) hit during expanded nightly sync window. New vendor onboarding tripled the number of warehouse location updates.
resolution_steps: Alert on warehouse-api sync failures >50 in 5 minutes
Checked Netsuite API response codes — 429 rate limit errors
Reduced sync parallelism from 10 to 3 concurrent requests
Implemented request batching (50 updates per request vs 1)
Reran failed sync batch successfully
Post-incident: requested Netsuite rate limit increase to 500 req/min

title: User profile service merge conflict on duplicate emails
root_cause: Profile merge logic failed when two accounts with same email but different OAuth providers (Google vs Apple) attempted to merge. Resulted in orphaned preference data.
resolution_steps: Support escalation: member lost dietary preferences after linking Apple account
Identified profile merge failure in application logs
Manually merged profiles using admin tool
Restored preferences from audit log
Post-incident: added OAuth provider conflict resolution to merge logic

title: Catalog search Elasticsearch OOM on complex faceted query
root_cause: Marketing landing page generated a query with 12 facet filters. Elasticsearch aggregation memory exceeded per-query limit, causing node to OOM and restart.
resolution_steps: Alert on catalog-search node restart
Identified OOM kill in node system logs
Found offending query in slow query log (12 facet aggregations)
Added query complexity limit (max 6 facet filters per request)
Increased ES node heap from 8GB to 12GB as buffer
Marketing updated landing page to use pre-computed filter sets

title: Redis cluster failover causing auth service blip
root_cause: Scheduled Redis cluster maintenance triggered automatic failover. User-auth client library didn't handle sentinel failover gracefully, causing 30 seconds of auth failures.
resolution_steps: Alert on user-auth error rate >1%
Confirmed Redis failover in cluster events
Auth errors auto-resolved after client reconnected to new primary
Post-incident: upgraded Redis client library with improved sentinel support
Added retry logic for transient Redis connection failures

title: Fulfillment router capacity miscalculation after DC expansion
root_cause: New distribution center added to warehouse-api but fulfillment-router configuration not updated. Orders eligible for new DC routed to already-full existing DCs.
resolution_steps: Operations flagged imbalanced order distribution across DCs
Traced to fulfillment-router DC config missing new NV-02 location
Updated routing configuration with new DC capacity and zones
Rebalanced queued orders to include NV-02
Post-incident: automated DC config sync from warehouse-api to fulfillment-router

title: Recommendation engine A/B test causing latency regression
root_cause: New recommendation model variant in A/B test consumed 2x memory and 3x inference time. 50% traffic split caused aggregate latency p99 to exceed SLA.
resolution_steps: Alert on recommendation-engine latency p99 >300ms
Identified A/B test traffic split as root cause via feature flag dashboard
Reduced test variant traffic from 50% to 10%
Latency normalized within 5 minutes
Post-incident: added latency guardrails to A/B test framework

title: Order service connection pool exhaustion during evening peak
root_cause: Connection pool to PostgreSQL exhausted during evening order surge. Slow query on order history endpoint held connections, preventing new checkouts.
resolution_steps: Alert on order-service connection pool utilization >90%
Identified slow query on order history (missing index on member_id + created_at)
Killed long-running queries via pg_terminate_backend
Added missing composite index (deployed via emergency migration)
Connection pool utilization dropped to 35% within 2 minutes
Post-incident: added query timeout of 5s for all non-batch DB queries
